reviews.text:
Some excellent staff, but systemic failures in care and ethics:
We had a polarised experience here. While some of the team (Maartje in nursing, Ruth in admin) and specific doctors like Dr. Amanda and Dr. Anna are fantastic, the clinic’s management and diagnostic approach were deeply disappointing and frankly frightening.

The positives: If you are assigned the team mentioned above, you are in good hands.

The concerns (shortlist out of many):

Lack of holistic investigation: Despite being 35+ and raising concerns on initial test results, our requests for further testing were dismissed. Its all put on the women when men are 50% of the equation. We felt pushed into expensive, repetitive cycles without investigating the unexplored causes. Don't rush, push to do thorough investigative work (not just surface level tests especially for both partners) before you start any rounds.

Financial priority over care: After an unexpected failed round where we had clear fertilisation issues, the doctor refused to answer clarifying questions via email, insisting on a £250 appointment fee. At such a vulnerable time, this felt exploitative. Our experience at other clinics is entirely different where Doctors' answer our questions and work with us not just talk at us.

Administrative negligence: Most distressingly, our embryos were moved even after we had explicitly withdrawn consent.

We spent upwards of £30k and felt like a transaction rather than patients. We have since moved to a clinic that prioritises investigation over rushing rounds. Please do your research before committing and avoid at all costs if you are above 35 and don't have either of those doctors.

Absolutely terrible experience. In our view, the team seemed unprofessional and disorganized, and we never felt genuinely cared for. Because they gave my partner sparkling water beforehand, something we were later told interfered with sedation, my partner ultimately had to undergo the procedure without sedation, which was extremely painful! Due to a miscommunication with my partner's prescription, giving unclear instructions on the dosage and then attempting to correct it through an improvised email. While they were very quick to request payment, they did not show the same urgency in properly preparing us for the extraction. Whenever we tried to voice concerns, we felt dismissed and treated as though we were the problem. Never again with these people!

I had my first consultation at the clinic and was set on freezing my eggs there. Dr Louisy was lovely and very reassuring. I was very keen to quickly progress my egg freezing journey with her.

I had a scan at the first consultation. This was on Wednesday 23 July 2025. I was told I would receive my egg freezing plan by the finance team. I chased for the egg freezing plan and received no response.

Today is 28 July 2025. I received an invoice for £500 for my consultation and scan. I have not, to date, received my egg freezing plan.

In response to the email containing the invoice, I asked when I would receive my egg freezing plan (after having already requested it the week prior).

In response, I was asked “We [sic.] should we expect payment for the invoice”. I had, in fact, already paid the invoice.

All of the above demonstrates to me that the clinic is more intent on receiving payment than progressing my care. I selected Aria because of the personalised experience it claimed to offer. It has not lived up to that service and I will be taking my custom elsewhere.

rubbish service from the start. I suspect those good reviews were created by themselves.

I made an appointment for a consultation, but they didn't make it clear whether it was online or offline. They even didn’t send me invoice after saying it’s confirmed. I asked them if it’s really confirmed they used more than one week to respond that the booking was successful. They didn't send me an invoice until the last moment before the consultation started and then repeatedly blamed me for assuming that the consultation was online. I have never encountered such rubbish service

Asked for a call to discuss the process, when they finally called me ( late) the woman I spoke to was incredibly rude and patronising. She was meant to send follow- up information and unsurprisingly based on her rude tone that never appeared.

i had bad experience bad service
i wouldn't recommend this fertility place.
they can not keep track of client's payments. doctors were very negative.

Hello, thank you for leaving a review. We are unable to find any record of your visit or interaction with Aria Fertility Clinic. If you have any concerns or feedback, we would really appreciate the opportunity to discuss them with you … More

My wife and I had five IVF cycles with one of the directors at Aria and ultimately felt disappointed. Although we did end up having success, it wasn’t thanks to Aria — we sought a second opinion from another clinic for our final round and only returned to Aria for the transfer because we had already paid and our embryo was stored there.
We wouldn’t recommend Aria overall. While the clinic itself is lovely and the staff are kind, lab is great too, the directors are not present, have too many clients, and aren’t able to provide a personalised experience. Like many of the larger private clinics in London, they just can't offer the one-on-one care you’d expect for the price you pay.

After completing an egg freezing cycle with Aria, I wanted to share my experience. Initially, I chose Aria due to its impressive presentation and welcoming atmosphere. My first interactions with several staff members were pleasant, setting …

We went to Aria clinic for a fertility MOT after struggling to conceive. The location and team were super easy and lovely. However first red flag was sending my husband’s results via email a week before a consultation without any context - …

Good Clinical Care but Very Poor Communication

We’re very grateful to Aria Fertility for helping us successfully freeze …
I've taken a bit of time to gather my thoughts so I can write this review as fairly and clearly as possible. …

I would not recommend Aria Fertility Clinic.
